description: ARIA 2D Materials PSP Knowledge Graph (demo, 28 relationships for tutorial). Intentionally includes complete P-S-P chains for MoS2 (enabling Tier 1), P-to-P shortcuts that cause contextual tunneling (demonstrating naive_kg harm), orphan edges (partial evidence), and WS2 analogical transfer edges (enabling Tier 2).

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: CVD growth at 750C on SiO2 substrate yields large-grain MoS2 with improved crystallinity relative to lower temperature synthesis.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: CVD growth at 750C on SiO2 substrate yields large-grain MoS2 with improved crystallinity relative to lower temperature synthesis.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: When Re is present at the growth front of a MoS2 grain, the energy of formation of edge sulfur vacancies is increased by ~1.4 eV, effectively suppressing vacancy formation.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: When Re is present at the growth front of a MoS2 grain, the energy of formation of edge sulfur vacancies is increased by ~1.4 eV, effectively suppressing vacancy formation.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Nb substitutional doping on Mo sites introduces shallow acceptor levels, increasing electron carrier concentration and enabling n-type conductivity.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Nb substitutional doping on Mo sites introduces shallow acceptor levels, increasing electron carrier concentration and enabling n-type conductivity.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Sulfur-rich growth atmosphere promotes stoichiometric MoS2 formation with S/Mo ratio approaching 2.0, reducing sulfur vacancies.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Sulfur-rich growth atmosphere promotes stoichiometric MoS2 formation with S/Mo ratio approaching 2.0, reducing sulfur vacancies.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Post-growth annealing at 300C in Ar atmosphere reduces point defect density by facilitating vacancy healing and lattice reorganization.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Post-growth annealing at 300C in Ar atmosphere reduces point defect density by facilitating vacancy healing and lattice reorganization.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Higher CVD temperatures promote lateral grain growth, resulting in grain sizes exceeding 10 um at 750C compared to ~1 um at 550C.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Higher CVD temperatures promote lateral grain growth, resulting in grain sizes exceeding 10 um at 750C compared to ~1 um at 550C.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Improved crystallinity reduces charged impurity scattering and interface phonon scattering, leading to carrier mobility exceeding 40 cm2/Vs in monolayer MoS2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Improved crystallinity reduces charged impurity scattering and interface phonon scattering, leading to carrier mobility exceeding 40 cm2/Vs in monolayer MoS2.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Increased sulfur vacancy formation energy reduces trap state density, improving field-effect mobility and drain current by nearly 10x in Re-doped MoS2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Increased sulfur vacancy formation energy reduces trap state density, improving field-effect mobility and drain current by nearly 10x in Re-doped MoS2.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Larger grain sizes reduce grain boundary scattering, enhancing the on/off current ratio to >10^7 in monolayer MoS2 FETs.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Larger grain sizes reduce grain boundary scattering, enhancing the on/off current ratio to >10^7 in monolayer MoS2 FETs.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Higher S/Mo ratio reduces sulfur vacancy concentration, directly decreasing interface trap state density that limits carrier mobility and subthreshold swing.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Higher S/Mo ratio reduces sulfur vacancy concentration, directly decreasing interface trap state density that limits carrier mobility and subthreshold swing.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Higher defect density increases phonon scattering, reducing thermal conductivity from ~52 W/mK (pristine) to ~30 W/mK in defective monolayer MoS2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Higher defect density increases phonon scattering, reducing thermal conductivity from ~52 W/mK (pristine) to ~30 W/mK in defective monolayer MoS2.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Nb substitutional doping increases electron carrier concentration, enabling stable n-type conductivity with carrier densities >10^12 cm^-2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Nb substitutional doping increases electron carrier concentration, enabling stable n-type conductivity with carrier densities >10^12 cm^-2.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Sulfur vacancies create mid-gap states that enable non-radiative recombination pathways, quenching photoluminescence in monolayer MoS2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Sulfur vacancies create mid-gap states that enable non-radiative recombination pathways, quenching photoluminescence in monolayer MoS2.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: CVD temperature 750C increases carrier mobility in MoS2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: CVD temperature 750C increases carrier mobility in MoS2.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Re doping at 0.1 at% increases carrier mobility in MoS2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Re doping at 0.1 at% increases carrier mobility in MoS2.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Nb doping at 1 at% enables n-type conductivity in MoS2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Nb doping at 1 at% enables n-type conductivity in MoS2.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Sulfur annealing reduces photoluminescence intensity in MoS2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Sulfur annealing reduces photoluminescence intensity in MoS2.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Monolayer MoS2 transitions from indirect to direct band gap (~1.8 eV) due to quantum confinement and absence of interlayer coupling.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Monolayer MoS2 transitions from indirect to direct band gap (~1.8 eV) due to quantum confinement and absence of interlayer coupling.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Hydrogen plasma treatment reduces contact resistance in MoS2 devices by phase-engineering the metal-semiconductor interface.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Hydrogen plasma treatment reduces contact resistance in MoS2 devices by phase-engineering the metal-semiconductor interface.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Strain engineering in monolayer MoS2 enhances piezoelectric response due to broken inversion symmetry.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Strain engineering in monolayer MoS2 enhances piezoelectric response due to broken inversion symmetry.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: CVD growth at 900C on sapphire substrate yields high-quality monolayer WS2 with large domain size.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: CVD growth at 900C on sapphire substrate yields high-quality monolayer WS2 with large domain size.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Nb substitutional doping increases electron carrier concentration in WS2, enabling n-type transport.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Nb substitutional doping increases electron carrier concentration in WS2, enabling n-type transport.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Optimized CVD synthesis produces stoichiometric WS2 with S/W ratio approaching 2.0, minimizing tungsten vacancies.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Optimized CVD synthesis produces stoichiometric WS2 with S/W ratio approaching 2.0, minimizing tungsten vacancies.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Surface potassium doping induces strong charge transfer and Fermi level shift, modifying the electronic structure of monolayer WS2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Surface potassium doping induces strong charge transfer and Fermi level shift, modifying the electronic structure of monolayer WS2.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Improved crystallinity in WS2 reduces non-radiative recombination centers, increasing photoluminescence quantum yield to near-unity for monolayers.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Improved crystallinity in WS2 reduces non-radiative recombination centers, increasing photoluminescence quantum yield to near-unity for monolayers.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Higher electron carrier concentration in WS2 enables stable n-type conductivity with on/off ratios exceeding 10^6.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Higher electron carrier concentration in WS2 enables stable n-type conductivity with on/off ratios exceeding 10^6.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: Higher S/W stoichiometry reduces tungsten vacancy concentration, decreasing interface trap state density in WS2 FETs.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: Higher S/W stoichiometry reduces tungsten vacancy concentration, decreasing interface trap state density in WS2 FETs.

causal_relationships.mechanism_quote: CVD temperature 900C increases photoluminescence quantum yield in WS2.
causal_relationships.evidence_text: CVD temperature 900C increases photoluminescence quantum yield in WS2.
